mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the t-statistic for the return difference is insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in consumer price inflation indicates robust demand growth without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict returns during recovery phases with Rank IC below 0.01, or positive return frequency falls below 52% in post-shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns in greater than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening construction demand and reduced household formation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in greater than 60% of high-inflation periods, or inflation fails to predict returns after controlling for federal funds rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during confirmed GDP expansion quarters.
